description: You are a baseball analyst analyzing MLB team payroll efficiency from 2000 to 2015 using the 'Lahman' database. Your objective is to determine if higher payrolls correlate with more wins and identify teams that overperformed their 'Pythagorean Expectation'. Using the 'Teams' and 'Salaries' tables, generate a dataset including Expected Win % (Runs^2 / (Runs^2 + RunsAllowed^2)), Expected Wins (Expected Win % * Games Played), Luck (Actual Wins - Expected Wins), and Cost Per Win (Total Payroll / Actual Wins). Save the complete analysis to /home/ga/RProjects/sabermetrics_analysis.R and the resulting dataset to /home/ga/RProjects/output/mlb_efficiency_data.csv. Additionally, identify the top 5 'unlucky' teams (lowest Luck) and the top 5 'efficient' teams (lowest Cost Per Win), saving them to /home/ga/RProjects/output/unlucky_teams.csv and /home/ga/RProjects/output/efficient_teams.csv respectively. Produce a scatter plot of Total Payroll vs Actual Wins featuring a linear regression smoothing line and a specific label for the 2002 Oakland Athletics, exported to /home/ga/RProjects/output/payroll_vs_wins.png.
